expected_effect: Reduce overpayment in toxic continuation by conditioning protection on queue-ranked inventory stress instead of a blanket continuation shell.
expected_failure_signature: If queue-rank gating still fires on transient continuation noise, time_weighted_mean_fee stays elevated, retail edge lags, and the branch replays queue-aware-stress-shell without enough mean-edge lift.
expected_win_condition: Retain or improve the anchor arb-loss improvement while lowering fee drag enough to lift mean edge into a promotable above-480 screen result.
hidden_coupling_removed: Inventory stress and continuation duration should not be treated as the same trigger for protection spend.
rationale: Use the apr18 shell and raw-survivor readouts as context, but spend one-sided protection only when inventory pressure and adverse-selection risk align strongly enough to resemble a back-of-queue state instead of paying a blanket continuation shell.
title: Queue rank protection budget
why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes when protection is eligible to spend based on a queue-ranked risk state instead of scaling the continuation shell coefficients.
